---



Looking at the logs, the core issue is clear:

```
OpenRouter error 401: {"error":{"message":"User not found.","code":401}}
```

This means your **OpenRouter API key is invalid or expired**. The bot IS hearing the user (transcription works fine - you can see "User said: ..." messages), but it can't generate a response because every call to OpenRouter fails with a 401 authentication error. That's why users say "I can't hear you" — the bot has nothing to say back.

**The fix:** You need a valid OpenRouter API key.

1. Go to [https://openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys)
2. Log in and generate a **new API key**
3. Update your `.env` file with the new key:

```
OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-v1-YOUR_NEW_KEY_HERE
```

4. Restart the bot

---

**Additionally**, the bot should handle this more gracefully — right now when the LLM returns `None` (due to the auth error), it silently does nothing, leaving the user in silence. Let me check how the response is handled: The code already handles the `None` case — when `first_question` is falsy, it just skips speaking. But the user gets no feedback that something went wrong.

**To summarize: Your OpenRouter API key (`sk-or-v1-35fefcdd...`) is returning "User not found" (401).** You need to generate a new valid key at [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys) and update the `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in your `.env` file. The transcription (Deepgram) is working perfectly — the bot hears and transcribes speech fine. It just can't respond because the LLM call fails every time.

---

_**User (2026-02-20 01:00-0800)**_

Can you improve anything in regards to how long it waits in between the person stopping their speech and the bot starting? It just doesn't feel like a real conversation and that's kinda the whole point.

  > Reduce TTS latency (streaming, pre-buffering, etc.)
- [ ] Pipeline-level optimizations
  > Overlap steps where possible (e.g., stream LLM into TTS)

<tool-use data-tool-type="task" data-tool-name="manage_todo_list">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **manage_todo_list** • Using "Manage and track todo items for task planning"</summary>

**Starting (2/6) *Optimize silence detection***

- [x] Audit current latency flow
  > Fully understand the pipeline: silence detection (1.5s wait + 0.2s check_interval) → stop recording → 0.1s sleep → extract audio → Deepgram REST transcription → LLM REST call → edge-tts synthesis → write temp file → FFmpeg play. Multiple serialized bottlenecks.
- [-] Optimize silence detection
  > Reduce silence_threshold from 1.5s to 1.0s. Reduce check_interval from 0.2s to 0.1s. These alone save ~0.5s.
- [ ] Optimize transcription (reuse HTTP session)
  > Reuse aiohttp.ClientSession instead of creating new one per call. Use Deepgram's pre-recorded endpoint with smaller params.
- [ ] Optimize LLM response (stream + reuse session)
  > Reuse aiohttp.ClientSession. Enable streaming so we can start TTS before full response arrives.
- [ ] Optimize TTS (stream to FFmpeg pipe)
  > Stream TTS chunks directly to FFmpegPCMAudio via pipe instead of writing temp file. Start speaking as soon as first sentence is ready.
- [ ] Pipeline: stream LLM → TTS → speak
  > Stream LLM sentence-by-sentence into TTS and start speaking the first sentence while the LLM is still generating the rest.

</details>
</tool-use>

self.min_speech_duration:\r\n                                # Too short - likely noise, reset and keep listening\r\n                                logger.debug(f\"Ignoring {speech_duration:.1f}s noise burst\")\r\n                                has_received_audio = False\r\n                                speech_start_time = None\r\n                                session.silence_start = None\r\n                                continue\r\n                            if not short_timeout:\r\n                                logger.info(f\"{silence_threshold}s silence detected, processing...\")\r\n                            break\r\n                    else:\r\n                        if session.silence_start is None:\r\n                            session.silence_start = time.time()\r\n                        elif time.time() - session.silence_start &gt;= no_response_timeout:\r\n                            if not short_timeout:\r\n                                logger.info(f\"No response for {no_response_timeout} seconds\")\r\n                            break",

Use provided session or create a new one\r\n            if http_session and not http_session.closed:\r\n                async with http_session.post(\r\n                    self.base_url,\r\n                    headers=headers,\r\n                    params=params,\r\n                    data=audio_data,\r\n                ) as response:\r\n                    if response.status != 200:\r\n                        error_text = await response.text()\r\n                        logger.error(f\"Deepgram API error {response.status}: {error_text}\")\r\n                        return None\r\n                    result = await response.json()\r\n            else:\r\n                async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\r\n                    async with session.post(\r\n                        self.base_url,\r\n                        headers=headers,\r\n                        params=params,\r\n                        data=audio_data,\r\n                    ) as response:\r\n                        if response.status != 200:\r\n                            error_text = await response.text()\r\n                            logger.error(f\"Deepgram API error {response.status}: {error_text}\")\r\n                            return None\r\n                        result = await response.json()\r\n            \r\n            return self._parse_response(result)\r\n            \r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            logger.error(f\"Transcription error: {e}\")\r\n            return None\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "    async def transcribe_audio(self, audio_data: bytes, mimetype: str = \"audio/wav\", http_session: \"aiohttp.ClientSession | None\" = None) -&gt; Optional[dict]:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Transcribe audio data using Deepgram's REST API.\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            audio_data: Raw audio bytes\r\n            mimetype: Audio MIME type (default: audio/wav)\r\n            http_session: Optional shared aiohttp session for connection reuse\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            Dictionary with transcript and segments, or None on error\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        if not self.api_key:\r\n            logger.error(\"Deepgram API key not configured\")\r\n            return None\r\n        \r\n        if not audio_data:\r\n            logger.warning(\"No audio data provided for transcription\")\r\n            return None\r\n        \r\n        try:\r\n            headers = {\r\n                \"Authorization\": f\"Token {self.api_key}\",\r\n                \"Content-Type\": mimetype,\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            params = {\r\n                \"model\": \"nova-2\",\r\n                \"language\": \"en\",\r\n                \"smart_format\": \"true\",\r\n                \"diarize\": \"true\",\r\n                \"punctuate\": \"true\",\r\n                \"utterances\": \"true\",\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            # Use provided session or create a new one\r\n            if http_session and not http_session.closed:\r\n                async with http_session.post(\r\n                    self.base_url,\r\n                    headers=headers,\r\n                    params=params,\r\n                    data=audio_data,\r\n                ) as response:\r\n                    if response.status != 200:\r\n                        error_text = await response.text()\r\n                        logger.error(f\"Deepgram API error {response.status}: {error_text}\")\r\n                        return None\r\n                    result = await response.json()\r\n            else:\r\n                async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:\r\n                    async with session.post(\r\n                        self.base_url,\r\n                        headers=headers,\r\n                        params=params,\r\n                        data=audio_data,\r\n                    ) as response:\r\n                        if response.status != 200:\r\n                            error_text = await response.text()\r\n                            logger.error(f\"Deepgram API error {response.status}: {error_text}\")\r\n                            return None\r\n                        result = await response.json()\r\n            \r\n            return self._parse_response(result)\r\n            \r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            logger.error(f\"Transcription error: {e}\")\r\n            return None",

  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n    async def _get_llm_response(self, session: InterviewSession, is_initial: bool = False) -&gt; Optional[str]:\r\n        \"\"\"Get a response from the LLM via OpenRouter (non-streaming, for backward compat).\"\"\"\r\n        full_text = \"\"\r\n        async for sentence in self._stream_llm_sentences(session, is_initial=is_initial):\r\n            full_text += sentence\r\n        return full_text.strip() if full_text.strip() else None\r\n\r\n    async def _stream_llm_sentences(self, session: InterviewSession, is_initial: bool = False):\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Stream LLM response sentence-by-sentence via OpenRouter SSE.\r\n        \r\n        Yields complete sentences as soon as they're ready, so the caller\r\n        can start TTS on the first sentence while later ones are still generating.\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        if not self.openrouter_key:\r\n            logger.error(\"OpenRouter API key not configured\")\r\n            return\r\n        \r\n        max_retries = 3\r\n        \r\n        for attempt in range(max_retries):\r\n            try:\r\n                messages = session.conversation_history.copy()\r\n                \r\n                if is_initial:\r\n                    messages.append({\r\n                        \"role\": \"user\",\r\n                        \"content\": f\"[SYSTEM: A practice user named {session.applicant.display_name} is ready for their STAR practice session. Ask your first behavioral question. Remember to keep it short since this will be spoken aloud. Do NOT greet them - they've already been greeted.]\"\r\n                    })\r\n                \r\n                http = await self._get_http_session()\r\n                async with http.post(\r\n                    \"https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions\",\r\n                    headers={\r\n                        \"Authorization\": f\"Bearer {self.openrouter_key}\",\r\n                        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\r\n                    },\r\n                    json={\r\n                        \"model\": self.openrouter_model,\r\n                        \"messages\": messages,\r\n                        \"max_tokens\": 200,\r\n                        \"temperature\": 0.7,\r\n                        \"stream\": True,\r\n                        \"provider\": {\r\n                            \"sort\": \"latency\",\r\n                            \"preferred_max_latency\": {\"p90\": 3.0},\r\n                        },\r\n                    },\r\n                    timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=15),\r\n                ) as response:\r\n                    if response.status == 520 or response.status &gt;= 500:\r\n                        error = await response.text()\r\n                        logger.warning(f\"OpenRouter error {response.status} (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries}): {error}\")\r\n                        if attempt &lt; max_retries - 1:\r\n                            await asyncio.sleep(1)\r\n                            continue\r\n                        return\r\n                    \r\n                    if response.status != 200:\r\n                        error = await response.text()\r\n                        logger.error(f\"OpenRouter error {response.status}: {error}\")\r\n                        return\r\n                    \r\n                    # Stream SSE tokens and yield complete sentences\r\n                    import re\r\n                    buffer = \"\"\r\n                    full_response = \"\"\r\n                    sentence_end_re = re.compile(r'[.!?]\\s')\r\n                    \r\n                    async for line in response.content:\r\n                        line = line.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"ignore\").strip()\r\n                        if not line.startswith(\"data: \"):\r\n                            continue\r\n                        data_str = line[6:]\r\n                        if data_str == \"[DONE]\":\r\n                            break\r\n                        try:\r\n                            chunk = json.loads(data_str)\r\n                            delta = chunk.get(\"choices\", [{}])[0].get(\"delta\", {})\r\n                            token = delta.get(\"content\", \"\")\r\n                            if token:\r\n                                buffer += token\r\n                                full_response += token\r\n                                \r\n                                # Yield complete sentences as they form\r\n                                while sentence_end_re.search(buffer):\r\n                                    match = sentence_end_re.search(buffer)\r\n                                    sentence = buffer[:match.end()]\r\n                                    buffer = buffer[match.end():]\r\n                                    yield sentence\r\n                        except (json.JSONDecodeError, IndexError, KeyError):\r\n                            continue\r\n                    \r\n                    # Yield any remaining text\r\n                    if buffer.strip():\r\n                        yield buffer\r\n                    \r\n                    # Update conversation history with full response\r\n                    if full_response.strip():\r\n                        session.conversation_history.append({\r\n                            \"role\": \"assistant\",\r\n                            \"content\": full_response.strip()\r\n                        })\r\n                    \r\n                    return  # Success, don't retry\r\n                        \r\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\r\n                logger.warning(f\"OpenRouter timeout (attempt {attempt + 1}/{max_retries})\")\r\n                if attempt &lt; max_retries - 1:\r\n                    continue\r\n                return\r\n            except Exception as e:\r\n                logger.error(f\"LLM request failed: {e}\")\r\n                return\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",

import asyncio
import logging
import io
import os
import json
import re

<summary>Tool use: **manage_todo_list** • Using "Manage and track todo items for task planning"</summary>

**Completed (6/6) *Pipeline: stream LLM → TTS → speak***

- [x] Audit current latency flow
  > Done.
- [x] Optimize silence detection
  > Done. silence_threshold 1.5→0.8s, check_interval 0.2→0.05s, added min_speech_duration filter.
- [x] Optimize transcription (reuse HTTP session)
  > Done. TranscriptionService accepts shared http_session, VoiceCog passes it.
- [x] Optimize LLM response (stream + reuse session)
  > Done. _stream_llm_sentences yields sentences via SSE streaming + shared HTTP session.
- [x] Optimize TTS (stream to FFmpeg pipe)
  > Done. Using pipe=True with io.BytesIO, no temp file. Poll at 0.05s.
- [x] Pipeline: stream LLM → TTS → speak
  > Done. _stream_and_speak speaks first sentence while LLM still generating rest. Text embed sent after full response.

</details>
</tool-use>

---



Let me first understand the full flow — from silence detection to transcription to LLM to TTS — to identify all the latency bottlenecks. Now I have a clear picture. The current pipeline is fully **serialized** with several wasteful delays:

1. **1.5s silence wait** + 0.2s poll interval → ~1.6s before processing starts
2. **New HTTP session** created for every Deepgram call → connection overhead
3. **New HTTP session** created for every OpenRouter call → connection overhead  
4. **Full LLM response awaited** before TTS starts
5. **Full TTS synthesis** before FFmpeg starts
6. **Temp file written to disk** before playback

Let me make this feel like a real conversation by: reducing silence detection, reusing HTTP connections, streaming LLM → TTS → playback in a pipeline. Now add the shared HTTP session helper right after `__init__`: Now update `_record_until_silence` to use the new faster settings and add minimum speech duration filtering: Now update the transcription service to accept an external HTTP session: Now update the transcription call in `_record_until_silence` to pass the shared session: Now the big one — rewrite the LLM call to use streaming and the shared HTTP session. This lets us start TTS on the first sentence while the LLM is still generating: Now let's add a streaming TTS-to-FFmpeg pipe method that avoids the temp file: Now the key optimization — add a `_stream_and_speak` method that starts speaking the first sentence while the LLM is still generating, and rewire the conversation loop to use it: Now rewire the main conversation loop to use `_stream_and_speak` for LLM responses: Now let's also remove the `tempfile` import since we no longer need it: Now remove the duplicate `import re` inside `_clean_for_speech` and `_clean_for_display` since we moved it to the top: Also remove the inline `import time as _time` since `time` is already imported at the top: Now let's verify the final file is clean: Let's do a final sanity check on the key sections: Everything looks clean. Let me also verify no unused imports remain: Good. Let me do a final check on the complete flow to make sure `_stream_and_speak` is compatible with the non-initial first question call: All optimizations are in place. Here's a summary of what was changed and the expected latency improvements:

---

## Changes Made

### 1. Silence Detection — saved ~0.7s
- **`silence_threshold`**: 1.5s → **0.8s** — detects end-of-speech faster
- **`check_interval`**: 0.2s → **0.05s** — polls audio 4x more frequently  
- **`min_speech_duration`**: new 0.3s filter to ignore noise bursts (prevents false triggers from the lower threshold)

### 2. Shared HTTP Session — saved ~0.2-0.5s per call
- Created a **persistent `aiohttp.ClientSession`** with TCP connection reuse (`keepalive_timeout=60`)
- Eliminates DNS lookup + TCP handshake + TLS negotiation on every Deepgram & OpenRouter call
- Passed to both transcription and LLM calls

### 3. LLM Streaming — saved ~1-3s (biggest win)
- Switched from waiting for **full response** to **SSE streaming** (`"stream": True`)
- New `_stream_llm_sentences()` async generator yields complete sentences as tokens arrive
- First sentence is available in ~0.3-0.5s instead of waiting 2-4s for the full response

### 4. Pipelined Speech — saved ~0.5-1s
- New `_stream_and_speak()` method **speaks the first sentence while the LLM is still generating** subsequent sentences
- Each sentence goes through TTS → voice immediately, no waiting for the full response

### 5. TTS Playback — saved ~0.1-0.3s
- Eliminated temp file writes — now streams MP3 bytes directly to FFmpeg via `pipe=True`
- Playback poll reduced from 0.1s → 0.05s

### Estimated Total Improvement
**Before:** ~4-6 seconds from user stopping speech to bot starting to respond  
**After:** ~1-2 seconds — should feel much more like a real conversation

> ⚠️ **Reminder:** Your OpenRouter API key is still returning 401 errors. You need to generate a new key at [openrouter.ai/keys](https://openrouter.ai/keys) and update your `.env` before any of this will work.

The error `Chunk too big` is coming from aiohttp's SSE streaming — the response chunks from OpenRouter's streaming API exceed aiohttp's default content read size. Let me look at the streaming code and fix it. The issue is `async for line in response.content` — this iterates over raw chunks from the TCP stream, not line-by-line. A single chunk can be huge or span multiple SSE lines. I need to use `response.content.readline()` or `iter_chunked()` with proper line-based SSE parsing. Let me verify the fix looks correct in context: The fix: replaced `async for line in response.content` (which reads raw TCP chunks of arbitrary size — causing "Chunk too big") with `response.content.iter_any()` plus manual line-buffering. This reads whatever bytes are available without size limits, then splits on `\n` to properly parse SSE `data:` lines. Restart the bot and it should stream correctly now.

Drop the transcript preview first\r\n    since it's least valuable.\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    def _embed_size(e: discord.Embed) -&gt; int:\r\n        total = len(e.title or \"\") + len(e.description or \"\")\r\n        if e.footer and e.footer.text:\r\n            total += len(e.footer.text)\r\n        if e.author and e.author.name:\r\n            total += len(e.author.name)\r\n        for field in e.fields:\r\n            total += len(field.name or \"\") + len(field.value or \"\")\r\n        return total\r\n    \r\n    # First pass: if over limit, drop the transcript preview field\r\n    if _embed_size(embed) &gt; max_total:\r\n        for i, field in enumerate(embed.fields):\r\n            if \"Session Preview\" in (field.name or \"\"):\r\n                embed.remove_field(i)\r\n                break\r\n    \r\n    # Second pass: truncate the longest field values until under limit\r\n    while _embed_size(embed) &gt; max_total and embed.fields:\r\n        # Find longest field\r\n        longest_idx = max(range(len(embed.fields)), key=lambda i: len(embed.fields[i].value or \"\"))\r\n        field = embed.fields[longest_idx]\r\n        current_val = field.value or \"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate how much we need to cut\r\n        overshoot = _embed_size(embed) - max_total\r\n        new_len = max(100, len(current_val) - overshoot - 20)\r\n        truncated = current_val[:new_len].rstrip() + \"\\n*…(truncated)*\"\r\n        \r\n        embed.set_field_at(\r\n            longest_idx,\r\n            name=field.name,\r\n            value=truncated,\r\n            inline=field.inline,\r\n        )\r\n    \r\n    return embed\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "    # Footer\r\n    embed.set_footer(\r\n        text=f\"STARCoach • Practice Session • Readiness Threshold: {fit_threshold}\",\r\n    )\r\n\r\n    # Enforce Discord's 6000-character embed limit\r\n    embed = _enforce_embed_limit(embed, max_total=5900)\r\n\r\n    return embed\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _enforce_embed_limit(embed: discord.Embed, max_total: int = 5900) -&gt; discord.Embed:\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    Trim an embed so its total character count stays under Discord's limit.\r\n    \r\n    Strategy: calculate total size, then progressively truncate the longest\r\n    fields until we're under the limit. Drop the transcript preview first\r\n    since it's least valuable.\r\n    \"\"\"\r\n    def _embed_size(e: discord.Embed) -&gt; int:\r\n        total = len(e.title or \"\") + len(e.description or \"\")\r\n        if e.footer and e.footer.text:\r\n            total += len(e.footer.text)\r\n        if e.author and e.author.name:\r\n            total += len(e.author.name)\r\n        for field in e.fields:\r\n            total += len(field.name or \"\") + len(field.value or \"\")\r\n        return total\r\n    \r\n    # First pass: if over limit, drop the transcript preview field\r\n    if _embed_size(embed) &gt; max_total:\r\n        for i, field in enumerate(embed.fields):\r\n            if \"Session Preview\" in (field.name or \"\"):\r\n                embed.remove_field(i)\r\n                break\r\n    \r\n    # Second pass: truncate the longest field values until under limit\r\n    while _embed_size(embed) &gt; max_total and embed.fields:\r\n        # Find longest field\r\n        longest_idx = max(range(len(embed.fields)), key=lambda i: len(embed.fields[i].value or \"\"))\r\n        field = embed.fields[longest_idx]\r\n        current_val = field.value or \"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Calculate how much we need to cut\r\n        overshoot = _embed_size(embed) - max_total\r\n        new_len = max(100, len(current_val) - overshoot - 20)\r\n        truncated = current_val[:new_len].rstrip() + \"\\n*…(truncated)*\"\r\n        \r\n        embed.set_field_at(\r\n            longest_idx,\r\n            name=field.name,\r\n            value=truncated,\r\n            inline=field.inline,\r\n        )\r\n    \r\n    return embed",
  "toolCallResult": null
}

int:\r\n        \"\"\"Create a placeholder interview row and return its ID.\r\n        \r\n        Called at session start so the interview_id is available\r\n        for display in text-channel embeds throughout the session.\r\n        The transcript is filled in later via update_interview_transcript().\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\r\n            cursor = await db.execute(\r\n                \"\"\"\r\n                INSERT INTO interviews \r\n                (applicant_id, applicant_name, guild_id, channel_name, transcript, started_at)\r\n                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\r\n                \"\"\",\r\n                (\r\n                    applicant_id,\r\n                    applicant_name,\r\n                    guild_id,\r\n                    channel_name,\r\n                    \"\",  # placeholder — filled later\r\n                    started_at.isoformat(),\r\n                ),\r\n            )\r\n            await db.commit()\r\n            interview_id = cursor.lastrowid\r\n            logger.info(f\"Created placeholder session #{interview_id}\")\r\n            return interview_id\r\n\r\n    async def update_interview_transcript(\r\n        self,\r\n        interview_id: int,\r\n        transcript: str,\r\n    ) -&gt; None:\r\n        \"\"\"Update the transcript and ended_at timestamp for an existing interview.\"\"\"\r\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\r\n            await db.execute(\r\n                \"\"\"\r\n                UPDATE interviews \r\n                SET transcript = ?, ended_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\r\n                WHERE id = ?\r\n                \"\"\",\r\n                (transcript, interview_id),\r\n            )\r\n            await db.commit()\r\n            logger.info(f\"Updated transcript for session #{interview_id}\")\r\n\r\n    async def save_analysis(self, interview_id: int, analysis: dict) -&gt; int:",
  "toolCallResult": null
}

  "content": "&#96;&#96;&#96;python\n# Keep backward-compatible alias that returns a single embed (first one)\r\ndef create_report_embed(\r\n    applicant: Any,\r\n    analysis: dict,\r\n    transcript_preview: str,\r\n    fit_threshold: int = 70,\r\n    interview_id: Optional[int] = None,\r\n) -&gt; discord.Embed:\r\n    \"\"\"Backward-compatible wrapper — returns the first embed.\"\"\"\r\n    embeds = create_report_embeds(applicant, analysis, transcript_preview, fit_threshold, interview_id=interview_id)\r\n    return embeds[0]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n",
  "textEditContent": "# Keep backward-compatible alias that returns a single embed (first one)\r\ndef create_report_embed(\r\n    applicant: Any,\r\n    analysis: dict,\r\n    transcript_preview: str,\r\n    fit_threshold: int = 70,\r\n    interview_id: Optional[int] = None,\r\n) -&gt; discord.Embed:\r\n    \"\"\"Backward-compatible wrapper — returns the first embed.\"\"\"\r\n    embeds = create_report_embeds(applicant, analysis, transcript_preview, fit_threshold, interview_id=interview_id)\r\n    return embeds[0]",
  "toolCallResult": null
}

<tool-use data-tool-type="task" data-tool-name="manage_todo_list">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **manage_todo_list** • Using "Manage and track todo items for task planning"</summary>

**Completed (5/5) *Add interview # to report embeds***

- [x] Add create_interview to database.py
  > Add a method to insert a placeholder row at session start (empty transcript) and return the interview_id. Also add update_interview_transcript() to fill in the real transcript at session end.
- [x] Add interview_id to InterviewSession
  > Add an interview_id: Optional[int] = None field to the InterviewSession class in voice.py
- [x] Pre-allocate interview ID at session start
  > In _run_conversation(), call create_interview() early to get the ID, store it on the session. Update _complete_interview() to use update_interview_transcript() instead of save_transcript().
- [x] Prepend interview # to text channel embeds
  > Update _speak_and_display() and _stream_and_speak() to show 'Session #XX' in embed footer. Do NOT change TTS speech text.
- [x] Add interview # to report embeds
  > Pass interview_id to create_report_embeds() and include it in the report title/footer.

</details>
</tool-use>

---



Let me first understand the current state of the codebase after your manual edits. Now I have the full picture. Let me also read the `embeds.py` to understand the report embed structure: Now I have the full picture. Let me plan and implement this. Now I'll add the two new methods to `database.py`: Now I'll pre-allocate the interview ID at session start in `_run_conversation()`, right after the system prompt setup and before the introduction: Now update `_complete_interview()` to use `update_interview_transcript()` instead of `save_transcript()` when we already have an ID: Now update `_speak_and_display()` to show the interview # in the embed footer: Now update `_stream_and_speak()` — the embed at the bottom of that method: Now update the report embeds. First, update `create_report_embeds()` in `embeds.py` to accept an optional `interview_id` parameter: Now update the `_make_embed` inner function and footer to include the interview ID: Update the footer in the single-embed path: Update the multi-embed footer for continuation parts: Update the final embed footer in the multi-embed path: Update the title fix at the end to include the ID: Now update the backward-compat wrapper too: Now update the callers in `voice.py` and `admin.py` to pass `interview_id`. First, voice.py's `_complete_interview`: Now check `admin.py` for calls to `create_report_embeds`: Let me verify that `interview_id` is available in that scope in admin.py: I see the issue — the variable is named `session_id_int` in admin.py, not `interview_id`. Let me fix that: Now let me verify everything looks correct by checking for errors: All done! Here's a summary of the changes:

### Changes Made

**`database.py`** — Two new methods:
- **`create_interview()`** — Inserts a placeholder row (empty transcript) at session start and returns the `interview_id`. This lets us display the ID throughout the session.
- **`update_interview_transcript()`** — Fills in the real transcript and sets `ended_at` when the session completes.

**`voice.py`** — Four changes:
- **`InterviewSession`** — Added `interview_id: Optional[int] = None` field
- **`_run_conversation()`** — Calls `create_interview()` right after system prompt setup, before the intro, so the ID is available immediately
- **`_complete_interview()`** — Uses `update_interview_transcript()` if we have a pre-allocated ID, falls back to `save_transcript()` otherwise
- **`_speak_and_display()` & `_stream_and_speak()`** — Embed footers now show `Session #XX • STAR Practice with ...` (TTS speech is untouched)

**`embeds.py`** — Report embeds updated:
- `create_report_embeds()` and `create_report_embed()` accept optional `interview_id` parameter
- Report titles show `STAR Coaching Report: Name (#XX)`
- Footers show `STARCoach • Session #XX • Readiness Threshold: ...`

**`admin.py`** — `!reanalyze` now passes `interview_id` to the report embeds too.

### How It Works
Every text channel embed during a session will show **`Session #5`** (or whatever the ID is) in the footer. The final coaching report will show the ID in both the title and footer. You can then use `!transcript 5` or `!reanalyze 5` to pull up that session.

**Created 4 todos**

- [-] Fix error handler in bot.py
  > Unwrap CommandInvokeError to log and display the actual error. This will help diagnose the !history issue.
- [ ] Fix !history to show session IDs
  > Add session # to each field in the !history embed so users know what ID to use with !transcript and !reanalyze.
- [ ] Fix !reanalyze missing arg message

{
  "invocationMessage": "Creating [](file:///e%3A/AI/STARCoach/test_embed.py)",

  "isConfirmed": {
    "type": 1
  },
  "content": "",
  "textEditContent": "import asyncio\nimport aiosqlite\nimport discord\nfrom datetime import datetime\n\nasync def test():\n    async with aiosqlite.connect('data/starcoach.db') as db:\n        db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n        cursor = await db.execute(\n            \"\"\"\n            SELECT i.*, ar.fit_score, ar.recommended\n            FROM interviews i\n            LEFT JOIN analysis_results ar ON i.id = ar.interview_id\n            WHERE i.guild_id = ?\n            ORDER BY i.created_at DESC\n            LIMIT ?\n            \"\"\",\n            (1426353112536580258, 10),\n        )\n        rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n        interviews = [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    if not interviews:\n        print(\"No interviews found\")\n        return\n\n    embed = discord.Embed(\n        title=\"📋 Recent Practice Sessions\",\n        color=discord.Color.blue(),\n        timestamp=datetime.utcnow(),\n    )\n\n    for interview in interviews:\n        session_id = interview.get(\"id\", \"?\")\n        score = interview.get(\"fit_score\") or \"N/A\"\n        ready = \"✅\" if interview.get(\"recommended\") else \"🔄\"\n        date = interview.get(\"created_at\", \"Unknown\")\n\n        print(f\"Adding field: name='#{session_id} — {interview['applicant_name']}', score={score}\")\n        embed.add_field(\n            name=f\"#{session_id} — {interview['applicant_name']}\",\n            value=(\n                f\"Readiness: **{score}**/100 {ready}\\n\"\n                f\"Date: {date}\\n\"\n                f\"Channel: #{interview.get('channel_name', 'Unknown')}\\n\"\n                f\"&#96;!transcript {session_id}&#96; · &#96;!reanalyze {session_id}&#96;\"\n            ),\n            inline=True,\n        )\n\n    embed.set_footer(text=\"Use !transcript &lt;#&gt; or !reanalyze &lt;#&gt; to view/redo a session\")\n    print(f\"\\nEmbed built successfully with {len(embed.fields)} fields\")\n    print(f\"Total chars: {len(embed.title or '') + len(embed.description or '') + sum(len(f.name) + len(f.value) for f in embed.fields)}\")\n\nasyncio.run(test())\n",
  "toolCallResult": null
}
```

</tool-use>

---



Let me investigate the `!history` command error and the related issues. The `get_recent_interviews` query looks fine. The issue is likely that the `!history` command's `embed.add_field` is failing because the placeholder rows we created with `create_interview()` have empty transcripts and no analysis — so they show up in results but may have `None` values causing issues. ### Changes Made

**`bot.py` — Improved error handler:**
- **`CommandInvokeError` unwrapping** — Now extracts the real exception and shows it: `❌ An error occurred: TypeError: ...` instead of the useless `❌ An unexpected error occurred.`
- **`MissingRequiredArgument`** — Now shows usage hint: `❌ Missing argument: session_id` → `Usage: !reanalyze <session_id>`
- **Added `CheckFailure`** and **`BadArgument`** handling

**`admin.py` — `!history` now shows session IDs:**
- Each field is now titled `#4 — chad | ConversaTrait` instead of just the name
- Shows quick-reference commands: `` `!transcript 4` · `!reanalyze 4` ``
- Footer reminds users of the commands
- Shows 📄 if transcript exists, ⏳ if placeholder (session in progress)

### About the `!history` crash
I couldn't reproduce the actual error — the DB query and embed construction both work fine locally. The improved error handler will now **show the real error message in Discord** next time it happens, so you'll see something like `❌ An error occurred: SomeException: details` instead of the generic message. I can see both issues clearly now:

1. **`!history` crash**: `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'id'` on `ctx.guild.id` — the command was run in DMs where `ctx.guild` is `None`
2. **Report embed >6000**: The old code at line 889 was sending `embed=embed` (singular) in a loop instead of individual embeds — meaning the multi-embed split we built wasn't being used because the bot was running the OLD code before your restart.

Let me fix both issues: Good — `voice.py` is already correct (iterating `for embed in embeds`). The log error at line 889 was from the **old** code before your restart. That's already fixed.

Now let me fix the actual bug — `!history` crashing because `ctx.guild` is `None` (DMs). I'll add `@commands.guild_only()` to all admin commands that need a guild: Now I can see all the commands that use `ctx.guild`. The fix is to add `@commands.guild_only()` to commands that need it. This includes: `!history` (line 34), `!session`, `!setrole` (line 243), `!status` (line 305 — uses `ctx.guild.id`). The commands `!transcript` and `!reanalyze` don't use `ctx.guild` so they're fine.

Also need to handle the `NoPrivateMessage` error in the bot error handler: Now add `NoPrivateMessage` handling to the error handler in `bot.py`: I need to add `NoPrivateMessage` **before** the generic `CheckFailure` catch since `NoPrivateMessage` is a subclass of `CheckFailure`: Both issues are now fixed:

### Issue 1: `!history` → `AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'id'`
The command was run in **DMs** where `ctx.guild` is `None`. Added `@commands.guild_only()` to all commands that need a guild context (`!history`, `!session`, `!setrole`, `!status`). Also added a `NoPrivateMessage` handler in `bot.py` so it shows a friendly message: *"❌ This command can only be used in a server, not in DMs."*

### Issue 2: Report embed exceeding 6000 chars
This was from the **old code** before your last restart (line 889 log). The multi-embed split we built previously is already in the current code and working correctly — `for embed in embeds:` sends each part separately. This is already fixed.

Restart the bot and both issues should be resolved.

_**Agent (model copilot/claude-opus-4.6)**_

Here you go:

---

**STARCoach — AI-Powered Interview Practice Bot for Discord**

STARCoach is a Discord bot that conducts live, voice-based behavioral interview practice sessions using the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result). Users join a voice channel, and STARCoach acts as a real-time interview coach — asking behavioral questions, listening to responses, and providing conversational coaching to help structure stronger answers.

After each session, STARCoach generates a detailed coaching report with a readiness score, STAR component breakdown for each answer, personalized strengths and improvement areas, and actionable tips — all delivered directly in Discord.

**Key Features:**
- 🎙️ **Live voice coaching** — Real-time conversation via voice with low-latency streaming responses
- 🧠 **AI-driven feedback** — Powered by Claude, with intelligent follow-ups that target missing STAR components
- 📊 **Detailed coaching reports** — Readiness scores, per-answer STAR breakdowns, and personalized improvement tips
- 📝 **Full session transcripts** — Every session is saved and retrievable for review
- ⚡ **Fully automated** — Just assign a role and join a voice channel to start practicing
- 🔁 **Re-analyzable sessions** — Re-run coaching analysis on any past session at any time

Whether you're preparing candidates for interviews, running a hiring community, or helping people level up their interview skills, STARCoach turns any Discord server into an on-demand interview practice lab.

---

Feel free to trim or adjust the tone to match your site.
